name: widgets-ui
description: \"Declarative UI widgets from JSON for React/Next.js from ui.inference.sh. Render rich interactive UIs from structured agent responses. Capabilities: forms, buttons, cards, layouts, inputs, selects, checkboxes. Use for: agent-generated UIs, dynamic forms, data display, interactive cards. Triggers: widgets, declarative ui, json ui, widget renderer, agent widgets,\"  dynamic ui, form widgets, card widgets, shadcn widgets, structured output ui
capabilities: Render declarative UI widgets from JSON definitions in React/Next.js
Generate layout components (rows, columns, boxes) with customizable styling
Create interactive form elements including inputs, textareas, selects, and checkboxes
Display data with typography components, badges, icons, and images
Handle user actions through callback handlers for buttons and form submissions
author: inference-sh-9
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. The static analyzer scanned 0 files (0 lines) and found 0 security issues. The skill provides declarative UI widget definitions in JSON format for React/Next.js rendering - there are no scripts, network calls, file system access, environment variable usage, or external command execution patterns.
valueStatement: Building dynamic user interfaces from AI responses is time-consuming and error-prone. The Widget Renderer skill enables Claude and other AI agents to generate rich, interactive UI components directly from structured JSON data.
limitations: Requires React/Next.js project with shadcn/ui components installed
Does not provide runtime API endpoints - only client-side rendering
The skill itself is documentation only; actual implementation requires installing the @/registry/blocks/widgets package
No built-in validation or data fetching - these must be implemented separately

useCases.title: AI-Powered Form Generation
useCases.description: Use Claude to generate complete contact forms, surveys, or data entry interfaces from JSON definitions based on user requirements.

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers creating data visualization interfaces
useCases.title: Dynamic Dashboard Cards
useCases.description: Create rich data display cards with gradients, icons, and structured layouts for analytics dashboards or user profiles.

useCases.title: Conversational UI Responses
useCases.description: Build interactive card-based responses in chat applications where Claude generates structured UI from agent outputs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Button
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple button widget JSON that says 'Click Me' with default styling and a submit action.

promptTemplates.title: Contact Form
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a contact form widget with name, email, and message fields plus a submit button. Use a column layout with consistent spacing.

promptTemplates.title: Flight Card
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a flight card widget showing departure (SFO) and arrival (JFK) airports with a plane icon between them. Use gradient-ocean background and rounded corners.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate a styled card with multiple components

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a dashboard widget with a header title, three stat cards in a row showing user count, revenue, and growth, and a recent activity list below. Use gradient-purple styling throughout.

bestPractices: Use consistent gap values (1-4) to maintain visual hierarchy in layouts
Leverage gradient backgrounds (gradient-ocean, gradient-purple, etc.) for visual emphasis on cards
Wrap interactive elements in appropriate layout components (row, col, box) for proper structure
antiPatterns: Avoid deeply nesting more than 4-5 levels - this creates complex JSON that is hard to maintain
Do not mix layout types inconsistently within the same component section
Avoid omitting the 'type' field - every widget object requires a type property

faq.question: How do I install the Widget Renderer components?
faq.answer: Run npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.inference.sh/r/widgets.json to add the widget components to your project.

faq.question: What React versions are supported?
faq.answer: The widgets work with React 18+ and Next.js 13+ using the App Router.

faq.question: Can I customize the styling of widgets?
faq.answer: Yes, widgets support background colors, gradients, padding, border radius, and spacing options directly in the JSON.

faq.question: How do I handle form submissions?
faq.answer: Pass an onAction callback to WidgetRenderer that receives the action type and formData when buttons are clicked.

faq.question: Are the widgets accessible?
faq.answer: Yes, the underlying shadcn/ui components include proper ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Claude can generate the JSON definitions directly from natural language requests.
